definitions: n. 持久性；长期性（在此语境下指职业生涯的持续时间）

examples.text: Not at all. It was hard to quit. I was tempted to stay in the game, because I love basketball and I loved my team. As for money, I turned professional at 18. So I'd actually earned a lot and saved most of it, because I had great financial advisers. I knew basketball wasn't a career with a lot of longevity for most players, so I wanted to change careers while I was still young.
examples.explanation: 这里的 longevity 指“持续时间长、能长久维持”。句子意思是：对大多数球员来说，篮球并不是一种能长期从事的职业，所以说话者想趁自己还年轻时转行。

reviewSessions.example_text: M: Not at all. It was hard to quit. I was tempted to stay in the game, because I love basketball and I loved my team. As for money, I turned professional at 18. So I'd actually earned a lot and saved most of it, because I had great financial advisers. [6] I knew basketball wasn't a career with a lot of longevity for most players, so I wanted to change careers while I was still young.
expected_definitions: n. 持久性；长期性（在此语境下指职业生涯的持续时间）
reviewSessions.feedback: 准确，对应此处“职业生涯的持久性/长期性”。
